title: Building a Real-Time Chat & Video Call Application
titleVi: Xây Dựng Ứng Dụng Chat & Gọi Video Thời Gian Thực
excerpt: How I built a cross-platform real-time communication app with WebSocket messaging, WebRTC video calls, Mediasoup SFU, and Spring Boot backend — supporting both Web and Mobile.
excerptVi: Quá trình xây dựng ứng dụng giao tiếp thời gian thực đa nền tảng với WebSocket, gọi video WebRTC, Mediasoup SFU và Spring Boot — hỗ trợ cả Web và Mobile.
content:
## Overview

Real-Time Chat Application is a cross-platform messaging and calling app inspired by Zalo, supporting private/group chat, multimedia sharing, video/audio calls, and AI-powered features on both Web and Mobile.

## Tech Stack

- **Web Frontend**: ReactJS with Vite
- **Mobile**: React Native with Expo
- **Backend**: Java 21, Spring Boot (Modular Monolith)
- **Database**: PostgreSQL with Flyway migrations
- **Cache & State**: Redis (OTP, online presence, session cache)
- **Real-Time Messaging**: WebSocket, STOMP
- **Video/Audio Calls**: WebRTC, Mediasoup SFU (Node.js)
- **Storage**: AWS S3, CloudFront CDN
- **Push Notifications**: Firebase Cloud Messaging (FCM)
- **Auth**: Spring Security, JWT, OAuth2
- **AI**: Spring AI, OpenRouter
- **Deployment**: Docker, Docker Compose

## Key Features

### 1. Real-Time Messaging
Private 1-on-1 and group chat powered by WebSocket and STOMP protocol. Messages show delivery status (sent → delivered → read) and typing indicators.

### 2. Video & Audio Calls
1-on-1 calls use WebRTC for peer-to-peer communication. Group calls are handled by Mediasoup SFU (Selective Forwarding Unit) for efficient multi-party video routing.

### 3. Cross-Platform Support
The app runs on both Web (ReactJS) and Mobile (React Native) with synchronized data and real-time updates across all devices.

### 4. Secure Authentication
Users can register with OTP verification via email, then authenticate using JWT. OAuth2 and remote device logout provide additional security.

### 5. Friend Management
Search for users, send/receive friend requests, build a friends list, and manage online/offline presence.

### 6. Push Notifications
When users are offline, Firebase Cloud Messaging delivers push notifications for new messages and calls.

### 7. AI Integration
Spring AI with OpenRouter powers a virtual assistant and conversation summarization features.

## Architecture

The backend follows a Modular Monolith pattern — each domain (messaging, user, call, notification) is an independent module with its own bounded context. This structure makes it easy to split into microservices in the future if needed.

Key architectural decisions:
- **WebSocket/STOMP** for bidirectional real-time messaging
- **Mediasoup SFU** for scalable group video calls (server-side media routing reduces client bandwidth)
- **Redis** for ephemeral state (online presence, OTP codes, session cache)
- **CloudFront CDN** for fast media delivery globally

## My Contributions

I developed the ReactJS web frontend including:
- Authentication screens (login, register with OTP)
- Main chat interface with conversation list and message panel
- Friend search and management UI
- WebSocket/STOMP integration for real-time message updates
- Video call interface with WebRTC

I also integrated REST APIs from Spring Boot and collaborated on testing the end-to-end messaging and calling flows.

## Challenges

Synchronizing message delivery status across multiple devices was complex, especially handling edge cases like network disconnection. The WebRTC + Mediasoup integration required careful coordination between the React client and Node.js SFU server for smooth group calls.

## Conclusion

This project showcases cross-platform real-time communication architecture combining WebSocket messaging, WebRTC video, and cloud infrastructure — all running on a modular Spring Boot backend with React clients.
